strengths: Strong Python and SQL hands-on experience
Built end-to-end ETL and data pipelines
Delivered measurable impact with dashboards and automation
Experience with Power BI/Tableau and visualization
weaknesses: Limited explicit ML/NLP production model examples
No clear client-facing financial services tenure
No explicit exposure to prompt engineering or retrieval workflows
improvement_bullets: Engineered predictive classification models for loan default risk using Scikit-Learn, performing feature engineering and model evaluation to ensure robust performance metrics.
Applied Natural Language Processing (NLP) techniques to categorize and analyze unstructured text data, improving information retrieval efficiency by 20% in internal project workflows.
Utilized Large Language Models (LLMs) and prompt engineering to automate the summarization of complex financial datasets, accelerating stakeholder decision-making cycles.

Partnered with cross-functional stakeholders to deliver client-facing analytical dashboards and actionable insights, ensuring alignment with high-stakes business objectives in a fast-paced environment.
Applied rigorous model governance and documentation standards, ensuring all analytical outputs are explainable, reproducible, and compliant with internal data security controls.
cover_letter: Dear Hiring Manager, I am excited to submit my application for the Data Science Quant Analyst opportunity with Howard-Sloan Search. I am interested in this opportunity because of the firm's commitment to bridging the gap between cutting-edge AI and high-impact financial advisory. What interests me about working for Howard-Sloan Search is its focus on building practical, scalable internal AI platforms that directly enhance banker productivity and client outcomes. I believe my rigorous quantitative background and my proficiency in building end-to-end data pipelines would make me a strong candidate for this role. During my professional career, I have approached problems by translating complex, ambiguous business questions into actionable analytical workplans. In fact, while at Shine Masters, I identified a need for more precise customer targeting and developed time series models to identify high-value segments, which contributed to a 300% increase in new customer acquisition over 12 months. This experience directly translates to the firm's need for analysts who can structure data to drive commercial results. Ultimately, I know that the Data Science Quant Analyst role at Howard-Sloan Search would allow me to bring my modeling and statistical analysis skills to support client-facing work. Additionally, my role as a Data Analyst at Shine Masters enabled me to develop my model evaluation and documentation skills. I spearheaded the transition from manual reporting to automated Power BI dashboards, documenting methodologies in a clear and auditable manner that reduced processing time by 40%. This commitment to governance and repeatable processes ensures the accuracy required for high-stakes financial environments. I’m excited by the opportunity to use my Python, SQL, and machine learning skills in working on complex client mandates at Howard-Sloan Search. I know that my strong quantitative instincts and my experience with modern data stacks align with what it takes to succeed at the Data Science Quant Analyst role. I would love the opportunity to discuss the role in more detail. Thank you for your time and consideration. I look forward to hearing from you soon. Warm regards, Justin Miller
